stories.name: Aisha
stories.status: Still going - we make it work
stories.story: We've been doing this for two years. The hardest part isn't the distance - it's watching him eat breakfast on FaceTime while I'm getting ready for bed. We exist in different days.

stories.name: Anonymous
stories.story: Three years of calls that dropped at the wrong moment. One flight that changed everything. Distance teaches you to say what you mean - you can't afford small talk when your time zones only overlap for two hours.

stories.name: James
stories.story: I learned her language. Not Korean - her language. The way she went quiet when something was wrong. I learned it too late, through a screen, and by the time I understood it she'd already moved on.
